items.md: # FOREWORD
items.value: FOREWORD

items.md: This Indian Standard (Second Revision) was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards, after the draft finalized by the Fire Safety Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.
items.value: This Indian Standard (Second Revision) was adopted by the Bureau of Indian Standards, after the draft finalized by the Fire Safety Sectional Committee had been approved by the Civil Engineering Division Council.

items.md: A series of Indian Standards covering fire safety aspects of buildings and different firefighting equipment and appliances have been published and these include a large number of terms relating to fire engineering, fire safety and fire technology. The use of some of the terms and the need to eliminate any ambiguity and misunderstanding in their interpretation had necessitated the preparation of standard on glossary of terms associated with fire safety. This standard was, therefore, brought out in 1978 to fulfil this need. A separate standard IS 7673 : 2004 ‘Glossary of terms for firefighting equipment (first revision) ’ has been published giving definitions of terms associated with firefighting equipment.

The use of some of the terms and the need to eliminate any ambiguity and misunderstanding in their interpretation had necessitated the preparation of standard on glossary of terms associated with fire safety. This standard was, therefore, brought out in 1978 to fulfil this need. A separate standard IS 7673 : 2004 ‘Glossary of terms for firefighting equipment (first revision) ’ has been published giving definitions of terms associated with firefighting equipment.

items.md: This standard published as glossary of terms associated with fire safety was first revised in 1999.
items.value: This standard published as glossary of terms associated with fire safety was first revised in 1999.

items.md: This second revision has been brought out to update the definitions based on the experience gained during these years as also to bring it in line with the National Building Code of India 2016 (SP 7 : 2016). In this revision, besides the modification of the existing definitions, number of new terms have also been included. In the formulation of this standard due weightage has been given to international co-ordination among standards and practices prevailing in different countries in addition to relating it to the practices in the field in this country.

items.md: # GLOSSARY OF TERMS ASSOCIATED WITH FIRE SAFETY
items.value: GLOSSARY OF TERMS ASSOCIATED WITH FIRE SAFETY

items.md: ### ( Second Revision )
items.value: ( Second Revision )

items.md: **1 SCOPE**
items.value: 1 SCOPE

items.md: This standard covers definition of general and safety terms relating to the phenomenon of fire, fire as related to building materials and structures and method of tests connected therewith and means of escape.
items.value: This standard covers definition of general and safety terms relating to the phenomenon of fire, fire as related to building materials and structures and method of tests connected therewith and means of escape.

items.md: **2 TERMINOLOGY**
items.value: 2 TERMINOLOGY

items.md: **2.1 Abnormal Heat** — Additional heat resulting from use up to and including, under normal conditions, that results in a fire.
items.value: 2.1 Abnormal Heat — Additional heat resulting from use up to and including, under normal conditions, that results in a fire.

items.md: **2.2 Accelerant** — Substance used to initiate and develop fire, for example, flammable liquids.
items.value: 2.2 Accelerant — Substance used to initiate and develop fire, for example, flammable liquids.

items.md: **2.3 Acceptance Criteria** — Criteria that form the basis for assessing the acceptability of the safety of a design of a built environment.
items.value: 2.3 Acceptance Criteria — Criteria that form the basis for assessing the acceptability of the safety of a design of a built environment.

items.md: **2.4 Activation Time** — Time interval from the response of a sensing device until the fire fighting/suppression systems, smoke extraction and the lime are fully operational.
items.value: 2.4 Activation Time — Time interval from the response of a sensing device until the fire fighting/suppression systems, smoke extraction and the lime are fully operational.

items.md: **2.5 Active Smoke Barrier** — Smoke barrier that moves from its retracted position into its fire-operational position automatically when called upon to do so.
items.value: 2.5 Active Smoke Barrier — Smoke barrier that moves from its retracted position into its fire-operational position automatically when called upon to do so.

items.md: **2.6 Agent Outlet** — Outlet in a piping system by means of which an extinguishing agent can be applied towards the source of fire.
items.value: 2.6 Agent Outlet — Outlet in a piping system by means of which an extinguishing agent can be applied towards the source of fire.

items.md: **2.7 Air-conditioning** — The process of treating air so as to control simultaneously its temperature, humidity, purity and distribution to meet the requirements of the conditioned space.
items.value: 2.7 Air-conditioning — The process of treating air so as to control simultaneously its temperature, humidity, purity and distribution to meet the requirements of the conditioned space.

items.md: **2.8 Alarm Time** — Time interval between ignition of a fire and activation of the alarm.
items.value: 2.8 Alarm Time — Time interval between ignition of a fire and activation of the alarm.

items.md: **2.9 Alternative Escape Route** — Sufficiently separated by either direction and space, or by fire resisting construction, to ensure that one is still available should the other be affected by fire.
items.value: 2.9 Alternative Escape Route — Sufficiently separated by either direction and space, or by fire resisting construction, to ensure that one is still available should the other be affected by fire.

items.md: **2.10 Ambient Temperature** — The temperature of the surroundings (unregulated outdoor temperature).
items.value: 2.10 Ambient Temperature — The temperature of the surroundings (unregulated outdoor temperature).

items.md: **2.11 Anoxia** — Severe lack of oxygen in the blood or the brain.
items.value: 2.11 Anoxia — Severe lack of oxygen in the blood or the brain.

items.md: **2.12 Arson** — Crime of setting a fire usually with a malicious intent.
items.value: 2.12 Arson — Crime of setting a fire usually with a malicious intent.

items.md: **2.13 Asphyxiation** — The suspension of respiration and animation, as the result of the inhalation of substances, such as carbon dioxide, methane, nitrogen, etc, when present in atmosphere to an extent sufficient materially to decrease its normal oxygen content.
items.value: 2.13 Asphyxiation — The suspension of respiration and animation, as the result of the inhalation of substances, such as carbon dioxide, methane, nitrogen, etc, when present in atmosphere to an extent sufficient materially to decrease its normal oxygen content.

items.md: **2.14 Assisted Evacuation** — Strategy that exists during which a designated person or persons provide assistance, during an emergency, to another person to leave a building or a specific part of the built environment and to reach a final place of safety.
items.value: 2.14 Assisted Evacuation — Strategy that exists during which a designated person or persons provide assistance, during an emergency, to another person to leave a building or a specific part of the built environment and to reach a final place of safety.

items.md: **2.15 Atrium** — A large-volume space created by a floor opening or series of floor openings connecting two or more stories that is covered at the top of the series of openings and is used for purposes other than an enclosed stairway; lifts hoist-way; an escalator opening; or as a utility shaft used for plumbing, electrical, air conditioning, or communications facilities.
items.value: 2.15 Atrium — A large-volume space created by a floor opening or series of floor openings connecting two or more stories that is covered at the top of the series of openings and is used for purposes other than an enclosed stairway; lifts hoist-way; an escalator opening; or as a utility shaft used for plumbing, electrical, air conditioning, or communications facilities.

items.md: **2.16 Auto-door Release** — A device when fitted to self-closing door allows to remain the door open under normal conditions but on the actuation of the alarm, releases the door which returns to the closed position.
items.value: 2.16 Auto-door Release — A device when fitted to self-closing door allows to remain the door open under normal conditions but on the actuation of the alarm, releases the door which returns to the closed position.

items.md: **2.17 Auto Ignition** — Ignition resulting from a rise of temperature without a separate ignition source.
items.value: 2.17 Auto Ignition — Ignition resulting from a rise of temperature without a separate ignition source.

items.md: **2.18 Auto-Ignition Temperature** — Minimum temperature at which a material will ignite by itself in air and sustain combustion without initiation by an external spark or flame under specified test condition.
items.value: 2.18 Auto-Ignition Temperature — Minimum temperature at which a material will ignite by itself in air and sustain combustion without initiation by an external spark or flame under specified test condition.

items.md: **2.19 Automatic Fire Detection and Alarm System** — A system comprising components and sub-systems required for automatically detecting smoke, heat or fire initiating an alarm and other actions as appropriate. This system also includes manually operated electronic fire alarm (MOEFA) system.
items.value: 2.19 Automatic Fire Detection and Alarm System — A system comprising components and sub-systems required for automatically detecting smoke, heat or fire initiating an alarm and other actions as appropriate. This system also includes manually operated electronic fire alarm (MOEFA) system.

items.md: NOTE — MOEFA system (with or without automatic fire detection and alarm system) includes all or some of the components such as manual call stations (initiating an alarm for fire and other actions as required), talk-back system and public address system.
items.value: NOTE — MOEFA system (with or without automatic fire detection and alarm system) includes all or some of the components such as manual call stations (initiating an alarm for fire and other actions as required), talk-back system and public address system.

items.md: **2.21 Balusters** — Plain moulded or turned posts for use of staircases, terraces and balconies (short pillars used as support to handrail of staircases).
items.value: 2.21 Balusters — Plain moulded or turned posts for use of staircases, terraces and balconies (short pillars used as support to handrail of staircases).

items.md: **2.22 Balustrades** — Assemblages of balusters or other ornamental treatment for forming a safeguard at the sides and the heads of stair flights and landing and also to terraces, balconies, etc.
items.value: 2.22 Balustrades — Assemblages of balusters or other ornamental treatment for forming a safeguard at the sides and the heads of stair flights and landing and also to terraces, balconies, etc.

items.md: **2.23 Barrier Movement** — Travel distance (for example, height drop) of an active barrier from its retracted position to its fire operational position.
items.value: 2.23 Barrier Movement — Travel distance (for example, height drop) of an active barrier from its retracted position to its fire operational position.

items.md: **2.24 Basement** — Any storey of a building wholly or partly below grade plane that is not considered the first storey above grade plane.
items.value: 2.24 Basement — Any storey of a building wholly or partly below grade plane that is not considered the first storey above grade plane.

items.md: **2.25 Blast Effect** — Damage caused by shock waves from an explosion.
items.value: 2.25 Blast Effect — Damage caused by shock waves from an explosion.

items.md: **2.26 BLEVE (Boiling Liquid Expanding Vapour Explosion)** — An explosion caused by rapid expansion of flammable gas stored in a container resulting in sudden release of huge quantities of atomised burning liquid which appear as a fire ball up and radiating intense heat all-around.
items.value: 2.26 BLEVE (Boiling Liquid Expanding Vapour Explosion) — An explosion caused by rapid expansion of flammable gas stored in a container resulting in sudden release of huge quantities of atomised burning liquid which appear as a fire ball up and radiating intense heat all-around.

items.md: **2.27 Boil Over** — In a flammable liquid storage tank, it is the discharge with exceptional violence of part of the contents of burning tank.
items.value: 2.27 Boil Over — In a flammable liquid storage tank, it is the discharge with exceptional violence of part of the contents of burning tank.

items.md: **2.28 Boiling Point** — Minimum temperature at which the vapour pressure of the liquid is equal to atmospheric pressure.
items.value: 2.28 Boiling Point — Minimum temperature at which the vapour pressure of the liquid is equal to atmospheric pressure.

items.md: **2.29 Brand** — A freely burning fragment of substance which may fall or become airborne either by wind or by convection currents.
items.value: 2.29 Brand — A freely burning fragment of substance which may fall or become airborne either by wind or by convection currents.

items.md: **2.30 Building** — Any structure for whatsoever purpose and of whatsoever materials constructed and every part thereof whether used as human habitation or not and includes foundation, plinth, walls, floors, roofs, chimneys, plumbing and building services, fixed platforms, verandah, balcony, cornice or projection, part of a building or anything affixed thereto or any wall enclosing or intended to enclose any land or space and signs and outdoor display structures. Tents, *SHAMIANAHS*, tarpaulin shelters, etc, erected for temporary and ceremonial occasions with the permission of the Authority shall not be considered as building.
items.value: 2.30 Building — Any structure for whatsoever purpose and of whatsoever materials constructed and every part thereof whether used as human habitation or not and includes foundation, plinth, walls, floors, roofs, chimneys, plumbing and building services, fixed platforms, verandah, balcony, cornice or projection, part of a building or anything affixed thereto or any wall enclosing or intended to enclose any land or space and signs and outdoor display structures. Tents, SHAMIANAHS, tarpaulin shelters, etc, erected for temporary and ceremonial occasions with the permission of the Authority shall not be considered as building.

items.md: **2.31 Burn** — Injury or damage caused by flame/heat to living beings.
items.value: 2.31 Burn — Injury or damage caused by flame/heat to living beings.

items.md: **2.32 Burn Back** — Flames going back over an area previously extinguished, may be due to incomplete cover or fire extinguishing media foam degradation.
items.value: 2.32 Burn Back — Flames going back over an area previously extinguished, may be due to incomplete cover or fire extinguishing media foam degradation.

items.md: **2.33 Burning Behaviour** — All the physical and/or chemical changes that take place when a material, product and/or structure burns or is exposed to fire.
items.value: 2.33 Burning Behaviour — All the physical and/or chemical changes that take place when a material, product and/or structure burns or is exposed to fire.

items.md: **2.34 Calorific Potential/Heat of Combustion** — Calorific energy which could be released by the complete combustion of a unit mass of a material.
items.value: 2.34 Calorific Potential/Heat of Combustion — Calorific energy which could be released by the complete combustion of a unit mass of a material.

items.md: **2.35 Cavity Wall** — A wall formed from/built into thicknesses, separated by an air space. The two thicknesses being connected by occasional ties of metal or brick. Also known as hollow wall.
items.value: 2.35 Cavity Wall — A wall formed from/built into thicknesses, separated by an air space. The two thicknesses being connected by occasional ties of metal or brick. Also known as hollow wall.

items.md: **2.36 Ceiling (Suspended, Fire-Resisting)** — A ceiling assembly capable of contributing, wholly or in part to the overall fire resistance of the floor above and/or its supporting members. This ceiling can be designed as a horizontal fire barrier to prevent the vertical spread of fire.
items.value: 2.36 Ceiling (Suspended, Fire-Resisting) — A ceiling assembly capable of contributing, wholly or in part to the overall fire resistance of the floor above and/or its supporting members. This ceiling can be designed as a horizontal fire barrier to prevent the vertical spread of fire.

items.md: **2.37 Channelling Screen** — Smoke barrier installed beneath a balcony or projecting canopy to direct the flow of smoke and hot gases from a room opening to the spill edge.
items.value: 2.37 Channelling Screen — Smoke barrier installed beneath a balcony or projecting canopy to direct the flow of smoke and hot gases from a room opening to the spill edge.

items.md: **2.38 Charring** — The formation of a light, friable, mainly carbonaceous constituent residual on wood or other organic matter resulting from incomplete combustion and/or devolatilization following exposure to heat.
items.value: 2.38 Charring — The formation of a light, friable, mainly carbonaceous constituent residual on wood or other organic matter resulting from incomplete combustion and/or devolatilization following exposure to heat.

items.md: **2.39 Chimney Effect (Flue Effect)** — The upward movement of hot fire effluent caused by the convection currents confined within an essentially vertical enclosure.
items.value: 2.39 Chimney Effect (Flue Effect) — The upward movement of hot fire effluent caused by the convection currents confined within an essentially vertical enclosure.

items.md: **2.40 Co-efficient of Thermal Expansion** — The proportional increase in length, volume of superficial area of a body per degree rise in temperature at a constant pressure.
items.value: 2.40 Co-efficient of Thermal Expansion — The proportional increase in length, volume of superficial area of a body per degree rise in temperature at a constant pressure.

items.md: **2.41 Column or Stanchion (Fire-resisting)** — A structural member (that transmits, predominantly through compression, the weight acting on top of it to other structural elements below) with or without any additional protection, capable of satisfying one of the criteria of fire resistance namely, resistance to collapse (stability), integrity and insulation during rise in temperature.
items.value: 2.41 Column or Stanchion (Fire-resisting) — A structural member (that transmits, predominantly through compression, the weight acting on top of it to other structural elements below) with or without any additional protection, capable of satisfying one of the criteria of fire resistance namely, resistance to collapse (stability), integrity and insulation during rise in temperature.

items.md: **2.42 Combustible** — An object that is capable of combustion.
items.value: 2.42 Combustible — An object that is capable of combustion.

items.md: **2.43 Combustible Material** — A material which either burns itself or adds heat to a fire, when tested for non-combustibility.
items.value: 2.43 Combustible Material — A material which either burns itself or adds heat to a fire, when tested for non-combustibility.

items.md: **2.44 Combustion** — Exothermic reaction of a combustible substance with an oxidiser, usually accompanied by flames, and/or glowing and/or emission of smoke.
items.value: 2.44 Combustion — Exothermic reaction of a combustible substance with an oxidiser, usually accompanied by flames, and/or glowing and/or emission of smoke.

**2.45 Common Path of Travel** — That portion of the exit access which the occupants are required to traverse before two (or more) separate and distinct routes or two (or more) exits become available. Common paths of egress travel shall be included within the permitted travel distance.
items.value: 2.45 Common Path of Travel — That portion of the exit access which the occupants are required to traverse before two (or more) separate and distinct routes or two (or more) exits become available. Common paths of egress travel shall be included within the permitted travel distance.

items.md: **2.46 Compartmentation** — The divisions of a building into fire-tight compartments by fire-resisting elements of building construction in order to contain a fire and smoke (products of combustion) within the compartment of origin for a specific period of time.
items.value: 2.46 Compartmentation — The divisions of a building into fire-tight compartments by fire-resisting elements of building construction in order to contain a fire and smoke (products of combustion) within the compartment of origin for a specific period of time.

items.md: **2.47 Compartment Floor** — A fire-resisting floor used in dividing a building vertically into separate compartments.
items.value: 2.47 Compartment Floor — A fire-resisting floor used in dividing a building vertically into separate compartments.

items.md: **2.48 Compartment Wall** — A fire-resisting wall used in dividing a building horizontally into separate compartments.
items.value: 2.48 Compartment Wall — A fire-resisting wall used in dividing a building horizontally into separate compartments.

items.md: **2.49 Conductive Floor** — Floor which is rendered electrically conductive by integral or applied floor finish for preventing static sparks.
items.value: 2.49 Conductive Floor — Floor which is rendered electrically conductive by integral or applied floor finish for preventing static sparks.

items.md: **2.50 Conflagration** — A fire which involves not only the building in which it originates, but also other buildings and property over a considerable area adjacent to it.
items.value: 2.50 Conflagration — A fire which involves not only the building in which it originates, but also other buildings and property over a considerable area adjacent to it.

items.md: **2.51 Consumable Power Supplies** — Any form of power that, when not available, prevents an active smoke barrier from moving to the required fire operational position.
items.value: 2.51 Consumable Power Supplies — Any form of power that, when not available, prevents an active smoke barrier from moving to the required fire operational position.

items.md: **2.52 Containment** — Restricting the spread of fire to surrounding structures or areas.
items.value: 2.52 Containment — Restricting the spread of fire to surrounding structures or areas.

items.md: **2.53 Convection** — Process of transfer of heat by movement of fluid or air.
items.value: 2.53 Convection — Process of transfer of heat by movement of fluid or air.

items.md: **2.54 Cooling** — A process of fire extinguishment control by reduction of temperature.
items.value: 2.54 Cooling — A process of fire extinguishment control by reduction of temperature.

items.md: **2.55 Corrosion Damage** — Physical and/or chemical damage or impaired function caused by chemical action.
items.value: 2.55 Corrosion Damage — Physical and/or chemical damage or impaired function caused by chemical action.

items.md: **2.57 Critical Fire Load** — Fire load required in a compartment to produce a fire of sufficient severity to cause failure of a fire barrier(s) or structural member(s) located within or bounding the fire compartment.
items.value: 2.57 Critical Fire Load — Fire load required in a compartment to produce a fire of sufficient severity to cause failure of a fire barrier(s) or structural member(s) located within or bounding the fire compartment.

items.md: **2.58 Cryogenic Gases** — Substances in a gaseous state which cannot be liquefied by pressure alone and are therefore cooled to a low temperature for storage and temperature in the liquid state.
items.value: 2.58 Cryogenic Gases — Substances in a gaseous state which cannot be liquefied by pressure alone and are therefore cooled to a low temperature for storage and temperature in the liquid state.

items.md: **2.59 Dangerous Contaminant** — A substance not normally found in a pure state and the presence of which even in small quantity may be dangerous owing to it acting as a catalyst or by itself entering into a potentially dangerous reaction.
items.value: 2.59 Dangerous Contaminant — A substance not normally found in a pure state and the presence of which even in small quantity may be dangerous owing to it acting as a catalyst or by itself entering into a potentially dangerous reaction.

items.md: **2.60 Dead End** — An area from which escape is possible in one direction only.
items.value: 2.60 Dead End — An area from which escape is possible in one direction only.

items.md: **2.61 Deflagration** — Combustion wave (explosion) propagating in subsonic velocity.
items.value: 2.61 Deflagration — Combustion wave (explosion) propagating in subsonic velocity.

items.md: **2.62 Deflection** — Movement of a smoke barrier when subjected to the buoyant force of the hot smoke, the movement of air, air pressure or any combination thereof.
items.value: 2.62 Deflection — Movement of a smoke barrier when subjected to the buoyant force of the hot smoke, the movement of air, air pressure or any combination thereof.

items.md: **2.63 Detonation** — Detonation of explosion and characterized propagating at supersonic velocity by a shock valve.
items.value: 2.63 Detonation — Detonation of explosion and characterized propagating at supersonic velocity by a shock valve.

items.md: **2.64 Discharge Rate** — Number of persons which can pass through one unit of exit width in a given time.
items.value: 2.64 Discharge Rate — Number of persons which can pass through one unit of exit width in a given time.

items.md: **2.65 Discharge Value** — The maximum number of persons who can effectively egress through a given number of units of exit width in a given period of time having regard, in multi-storey buildings, to the capacity of the staircase(s).
items.value: 2.65 Discharge Value — The maximum number of persons who can effectively egress through a given number of units of exit width in a given period of time having regard, in multi-storey buildings, to the capacity of the staircase(s).

items.md: **2.66 Door Assembly, Fire-Check/Fire Door** — Any combination of fire door, frame, hardware and other accessories that together provide a specific fire resistant rating to the opening in terms of its stability, integrity and insulation properties (in case of insulated doors), when installed in the openings in fire separation walls. Fire door is a component of fire door assembly.

items.md: **2.101 Fire Fighting Shaft** — An enclosed shaft having protected area of 120 min fire resistance rating comprising protected lobby, staircase and fireman’s lift, connected directly to exit discharge or through exit passageway with 120 min fire resistant wall at the level of exit discharge to the exit discharge. These shall also serve the purpose of exit requirement/strategy for the occupants. The respective floors shall be approachable from fire-fighting shaft enabling the fire fighters to access the floor and also enabling the fire fighters to assist in evacuation through fireman’s lift. The firefighting shaft shall be equipped with 120 min fire doors. It shall be equipped with firemen talk back, wet riser and landing valve in its lobby, to fight fire by fire fighters (*see Fig. 2 for a typical firefighting shaft.*)

items.md: **2.124 Fire Separating Wall** — A wall or walls that create fire separation between two tenants or dwelling units. Such walls include, tenant separation walls, corridor walls, vertical shafts, room separations. Walls that separate hotel room from the corridor or adjoining room are also fire separation walls. Fire separation walls are used to contain fire to the hotel room or the dwelling unit where fire has originated. Unlike fire walls, these walls can be added or changed during interior design project; and these walls do not necessarily extend from basement to roof and extend beyond roof.

Fire separation walls are used to contain fire to the hotel room or the dwelling unit where fire has originated. Unlike fire walls, these walls can be added or changed during interior design project; and these walls do not necessarily extend from basement to roof and extend beyond roof.

items.md: **2.125 Fire Separation** — The distance in metre, measured from the external wall of the building concerned to the external wall of any other building on the site, or from other site, or from the opposite side of street or other public space for the purpose of preventing the spread of fire.

items.md: ## 2.148 Horizontal Exit
items.value: 2.148 Horizontal Exit

items.md: A defend in place or a staging arrangement, providing safety from fire and smoke originating from the area of incidence, by allowing alternative egress from a compartment to an area of refuge or another compartment at or near the same level. This also includes such egress from a compartment to an adjoining building (see 2.83). A horizontal exit shall be through a fire door of 120 min rating in a fire resistant wall. Horizontal exit require separation with the refuge area or adjoining compartment through 120 min fire barrier. The adjoining compartment of the horizontal exit should allow unlocked and ease of egress and exits for the occupants using defend in place strategy.

items.value: 2.217 Roof (External Fire Exposure, Resistance, etc) — The ability of a roof deck and covering to resist both penetration by external fire and flame spread over the external surfaces.

items.md: **2.218 Roof Screen or Roof Curtain Boards** — A vertical screen or substantial non-combustible material fitted internally to the roof of a building to divide the roof into bays so that smoke and hot gases from a fire are contained within the bay of origin if used in conjunction with automatic fire vents, their early actuation is effected.
items.value: 2.218 Roof Screen or Roof Curtain Boards — A vertical screen or substantial non-combustible material fitted internally to the roof of a building to divide the roof into bays so that smoke and hot gases from a fire are contained within the bay of origin if used in conjunction with automatic fire vents, their early actuation is effected.

items.md: **2.219 Roof Venting** — A system of vents which will open automatically in the event of a fire and allow the escape of smoke and hot gases.
items.value: 2.219 Roof Venting — A system of vents which will open automatically in the event of a fire and allow the escape of smoke and hot gases.

items.md: **2.220 Roof Exits** — A means of escape on to the roof of a building, where the roof has access to it from the ground through alternative stair case or adjacent building.
items.value: 2.220 Roof Exits — A means of escape on to the roof of a building, where the roof has access to it from the ground through alternative stair case or adjacent building.

items.md: **2.221 Room Access** — A room which forms the only escape route from an inner room.
items.value: 2.221 Room Access — A room which forms the only escape route from an inner room.

items.md: **2.222 Secondary Fire** — A fire which has started some distance from the seat of the original fire but is due to the latter.
items.value: 2.222 Secondary Fire — A fire which has started some distance from the seat of the original fire but is due to the latter.

items.md: **2.223 Self-Extinguishing** — Incapable of undergoing sustained combustion after removal of the external source of heat.
items.value: 2.223 Self-Extinguishing — Incapable of undergoing sustained combustion after removal of the external source of heat.

items.md: **2.224 Self-heating** — An exothermic reaction occurring without the application of external heat.
items.value: 2.224 Self-heating — An exothermic reaction occurring without the application of external heat.

items.value: 2.225 Separating Element — Physical barrier intended to resist the passage of fire from one side of the barrier to the other side.

items.md: **2.226 Shaft, Fire-resisting** — A space bounded by fire-resisting elements of building construction and intended for the passage of persons, services or things.
items.value: 2.226 Shaft, Fire-resisting — A space bounded by fire-resisting elements of building construction and intended for the passage of persons, services or things.

items.value: 2.227 Shutter Assembly, Fire-resisting — Shutter which, together with its frame; is capable of satisfying the criteria of fire resistance with respect to collapse and flame penetration.

items.value: 2.228 Sleeping Risk — A form of occupancy or a part of a building in which bedroom or dormitory accommodation predominates, such as in hotels, boarding schools, hospitals and similar establishments.

items.value: 2.229 Smoke — Visible suspension in atmosphere of solid and/or liquid particles resulting from combustion or pyrolysis.

items.value: 2.231 Smoke and Heat Exhaust Ventilation System (SHEVS) — Set of components jointly selected to exhaust smoke and heat in order to establish a buoyant layer of warm smoke above cooler, cleaner air.

items.md: **2.232 Smoke Barrier** — A continuous membrane, or a membrane, where such membrane is designed and constructed to restrict the movement of smoke. It also refers to a device to channel, contain and/or prevent the migration of smoke (fire effluent).
items.value: 2.232 Smoke Barrier — A continuous membrane, or a membrane, where such membrane is designed and constructed to restrict the movement of smoke. It also refers to a device to channel, contain and/or prevent the migration of smoke (fire effluent).

items.value: NOTE — Smoke barriers can also be referred to as smoke curtains, smoke blinds or smoke screens.

items.md: Amendments are issued to standards as the need arises on the basis of comments. Standards are also reviewed periodically; a standard along with amendments is reaffirmed when such review indicates that no changes are needed; if the review indicates that changes are needed, it is taken up for revision.
